slides.title: The sea vegetable behind a *long healthspan*.
slides.sub: Seaweed is a quiet staple of Japanese coastal eating. Here is why it earns its place on the plate.

slides.title: A coastal eating pattern
slides.body: Along the Japanese coast, seaweed shows up daily in soups, salads and rice. It is part of *one of the longest-lived diets*.

slides.title: Iodine for your thyroid
slides.body: Seaweed is one of the richest natural sources of iodine, a mineral your thyroid needs to *help regulate your metabolism*.

slides.title: Soluble fiber that feeds you
slides.body: Its soluble fiber can support steady digestion and feed gut bacteria, which *may help* with fullness and blood sugar balance.

slides.title: Fucoxanthin, the brown carotenoid
slides.body: The pigment that tints brown seaweed is fucoxanthin, a carotenoid studied for how it *may support healthy metabolism*.

slides.title: Easy ways to add it
slides.body: Crumble nori over rice, stir wakame into miso soup, or toss a small seaweed salad. A little, *added often*, is enough.

slides.title: Turn small habits into *real momentum*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs your food, sleep, movement and steps, then turns them into one daily Health Score so good habits actually stick.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Seaweed is the quiet staple behind one of the world's longest-lived diets. 🌊🌿

Iodine for your thyroid, soluble fiber for your gut, and fucoxanthin, the carotenoid that gives brown seaweed its color. A small habit with a long history. Save this. 🔖
